title: nicotiana occidentalis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Nicotiana occidentalis subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western tobacco or wild tobacco, is a plant species native to North America, belonging to the nightshade family and containing nicotine. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, to treat respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis. The health benefits of Nicotiana occidentalis subsp. occidentalis include relief from congestion, improved lung function, and reduced inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and ease respiratory discomfort.
